# Architectural Drawing Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint drawn on aged, cream-colored paper showing detailed cross-sectional views of a Victorian-era building. The main drawing occupies the lower two-thirds of the page and displays a "Longitudinal Section" (labeled at bottom) of an ornate structure with multiple stories featuring pitched roofs with cupolas, numerous windows arranged in rows, decorative railings, columns, and internal floor divisions. The building shows varied architectural elements including a prominent tower with a spire on the left side, multiple gabled sections, and interior stairs. A smaller detailed section drawing labeled "Staircase Section" appears in the upper right corner, showing the internal construction of stairs and structural framing. Handwritten annotations appear throughout the drawing with measurement notations and construction details in brown ink. At the bottom of the longitudinal section, the label reads "Longitudinal Section" and references "B.D.Bagwell & Sampson" along with what appears to be an address. A color reference strip appears along the right edge of the paper. The drawing is rendered in sepia-toned ink with cross-hatching used to indicate solid areas and structural elements.

# Architectural Floor Plan - Second Floor

This is a historical architectural blueprint showing the second floor layout of a building designed by "F. McLoughsall & Son, Archts." located at "530 Pine Str S.F." (San Francisco). The document is drawn to a scale of "Eight Feet to One Inch" as noted at the bottom left.

The floor plan shows a symmetrical layout with multiple rooms labeled throughout. The upper portion features two large rooms labeled "Parlor/Bedroom" (each approximately 19'6" × 23'6") connected by a central space with mechanical fixtures. Below these are two "Bed Rooms" (approximately 11'2" × 14') flanking a central hall and staircase area. The lower section displays a more complex arrangement with numerous smaller rooms labeled as "Bed Room," "Sitting Room," and other spaces, including hallways and stairs marked with numerical room indicators (1, 2, 3, 4, 5).

Interior walls are rendered in light tan/brown ink lines showing doorways, room divisions, and circulation patterns. The paper itself shows significant aging with brown stains, discoloration, and deterioration throughout. A color reference scale appears at the top of the document for photographic documentation purposes.

520__a-1: This is a historical architectural floor plan drawn on aged paper, showing the First Floor layout of a residential building designed by E. M. Douglall & Son Architects, located at 330 Pine Street, San Francisco. The plan is rendered in sepia-toned ink on a cream-colored paper with visible stains, discoloration, and age-related wear. A measurement scale and color reference palette appear at the top of the document. The floor plan displays a symmetrical layout with multiple rooms labeled throughout, including a central Hall area, Old Ladies' Dining Room and Children's Dining Room on either side, various Bed Rooms scattered throughout, a Kitchen, Parlor, and other service spaces. Rooms include detailed dimensions marked in feet and inches. Doorways, windows, staircases, and architectural features are indicated with lines and symbols characteristic of traditional architectural drawing. The plan demonstrates a complex, multi-wing structure with distinct functional areas, typical of institutional or large residential architecture from the early 20th century. Handwritten notations and measurements are scattered throughout the drawing, and the document's edges are irregular and worn, suggesting considerable age and handling.
